candidates.abstract_snippet: Despite the promise of Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models as generalist robotic controllers, their robustness against perceptual noise and environmental variations in out-of-distribution (OOD) tasks remains fundamentally limited by the absence of long-term memory, causal failure attribution, and dy...

candidates.title: Drive My Way: Preference Alignment of Vision-Language-Action Model for Personalized Driving
candidates.abstract_snippet: Human driving behavior is inherently personal, which is shaped by long-term habits and influenced by short-term intentions. candidates.title: LaMP: Learning Vision-Language-Action Policies with 3D Scene Flow as Latent Motion Prior
candidates.abstract_snippet: We introduce \textbf{LaMP}, a dual-expert Vision-Language-Action framework that embeds dense 3D scene flow as a latent motion prior for robotic manipulation. candidates.title: MMaDA-VLA: Large Diffusion Vision-Language-Action Model with Unified Multi-Modal Instruction and Generation
candidates.abstract_snippet: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models aim to control robots for manipulation from visual observations and natural-language instructions. However, existing hierarchical and autoregressive paradigms often introduce architectural overhead, suffer from temporal inconsistency and long-horizon error accumul...

candidates.title: Do World Action Models Generalize Better than VLAs? A Robustness Study
candidates.abstract_snippet: Robot action planning in the real world is challenging as it requires not only understanding the current state of the environment but also predicting how it will evolve in response to actions. candidates.title: TAG: Target-Agnostic Guidance for Stable Object-Centric Inference in Vision-Language-Action Models
candidates.abstract_snippet: Vision--Language--Action (VLA) policies have shown strong progress in mapping language instructions and visual observations to robotic actions, yet their reliability degrades in cluttered scenes with distractors. candidates.title: SABER: A Stealthy Agentic Black-Box Attack Framework for Vision-Language-Action Models
candidates.abstract_snippet: Vision-language-action (VLA) models enable robots to follow natural-language instructions grounded in visual observations, but the instruction channel also introduces a critical vulnerability: small textual perturbations can alter downstream robot behavior. candidates.title: StageCraft: Execution Aware Mitigation of Distractor and Obstruction Failures in VLA Models
candidates.abstract_snippet: Large scale pre-training on text and image data along with diverse robot demonstrations has helped Vision Language Action models (VLAs) to generalize to novel tasks, objects and scenes. candidates.title: Not All Features Are Created Equal: A Mechanistic Study of Vision-Language-Action Models
candidates.abstract_snippet: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models combine perception, language, and motor control in a single architecture, yet how they translate multimodal inputs into actions remains poorly understood. candidates.title: Sparse Autoencoders Reveal Interpretable and Steerable Features in VLA Models
candidates.abstract_snippet: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have emerged as a promising approach for general-purpose robot manipulation. candidates.title: Shifting Uncertainty to Critical Moments: Towards Reliable Uncertainty Quantification for VLA Model
candidates.abstract_snippet: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models enable general-purpose robotic policies by mapping visual observations and language instructions to low-level actions, but they often lack reliable introspection. candidates.title: vla-eval: A Unified Evaluation Harness for Vision-Language-Action Models
candidates.abstract_snippet: Vision Language Action VLA models are typically evaluated using per benchmark scripts maintained independently by each model repository, leading to duplicated code, dependency conflicts, and underspecified protocols. candidates.title: ROBOGATE: Adaptive Failure Discovery for Safe Robot Policy Deployment via Two-Stage Boundary-Focused Sampling
candidates.abstract_snippet: Deploying learned robot manipulation policies in industrial settings requires rigorous pre-deployment validation, yet exhaustive testing across high-dimensional parameter spaces is intractable. candidates.title: Towards the Vision-Sound-Language-Action Paradigm: The HEAR Framework for Sound-Centric Manipulation
candidates.abstract_snippet: While recent Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have begun to incorporate audio, they typically treat sound as static pre-execution prompts or focus exclusively on human speech. candidates.abstract_snippet: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models leverage Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) for robotic control, but recent studies reveal that MLLMs exhibit limited spatial intelligence due to training predominantly on 2D data, resulting in inadequate 3D perception for manipulation tasks. candidates.title: CaP-X: A Framework for Benchmarking and Improving Coding Agents for Robot Manipulation
candidates.abstract_snippet: "Code-as-Policy" considers how executable code can complement data-intensive Vision-Language-Action (VLA) methods, yet their effectiveness as autonomous controllers for embodied manipulation remains underexplored. candidates.title: RoboECC: Multi-Factor-Aware Edge-Cloud Collaborative Deployment for VLA Models
candidates.abstract_snippet: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models are mainstream in embodied intelligence but face high inference costs. candidates.title: FASTER: Rethinking Real-Time Flow VLAs
candidates.abstract_snippet: Real-time execution is crucial for deploying Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models in the physical world. candidates.title: MultihopSpatial: Multi-hop Compositional Spatial Reasoning Benchmark for Vision-Language Model
candidates.abstract_snippet: Spatial reasoning is foundational for Vision-Language Models (VLMs), particularly when deployed as Vision-Language-Action (VLA) agents in physical environments. candidates.title: HeiSD: Hybrid Speculative Decoding for Embodied Vision-Language-Action Models with Kinematic Awareness
candidates.abstract_snippet: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) Models have become the mainstream solution for robot control, but suffer from slow inference speeds. candidates.title: Towards Generalizable Robotic Manipulation in Dynamic Environments
candidates.abstract_snippet: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models excel in static manipulation but struggle in dynamic environments with moving targets. candidates.title: AnoleVLA: Lightweight Vision-Language-Action Model with Deep State Space Models for Mobile Manipulation
candidates.abstract_snippet: In this study, we address the problem of language-guided robotic manipulation, where a robot is required to manipulate a wide range of objects based on visual observations and natural language instructions. candidates.title: OxyGen: Unified KV Cache Management for Vision-Language-Action Models under Multi-Task Parallelism

candidates.title: LILAC: Language-Conditioned Object-Centric Optical Flow for Open-Loop Trajectory Generation
candidates.abstract_snippet: We address language-conditioned robotic manipulation using flow-based trajectory generation, which enables training on human and web videos of object manipulation and requires only minimal embodiment-specific data. candidates.title: Agile-VLA: Few-Shot Industrial Pose Rectification via Implicit Affordance Anchoring
candidates.abstract_snippet: Deploying Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models on resource-constrained edge platforms encounters a fundamental conflict between high-latency semantic inference and the high-frequency control required for dynamic manipulation. candidates.title: SG-VLA: Learning Spatially-Grounded Vision-Language-Action Models for Mobile Manipulation
candidates.abstract_snippet: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models show promise for robotic control, yet performance in complex household environments remains sub-optimal. candidates.title: HUGE-Bench: A Benchmark for High-Level UAV Vision-Language-Action Tasks

candidates.title: Scaling Sim-to-Real Reinforcement Learning for Robot VLAs with Generative 3D Worlds
candidates.abstract_snippet: The strong performance of large vision-language models (VLMs) trained with reinforcement learning (RL) has motivated similar approaches for fine-tuning vision-language-action (VLA) models in robotics. candidates.title: HapticVLA: Contact-Rich Manipulation via Vision-Language-Action Model without Inference-Time Tactile Sensing

candidates.title: NavGSim: High-Fidelity Gaussian Splatting Simulator for Large-Scale Navigation
candidates.abstract_snippet: Simulating realistic environments for robots is widely recognized as a critical challenge in robot learning, particularly in terms of rendering and physical simulation. candidates.title: Tactile Modality Fusion for Vision-Language-Action Models
candidates.abstract_snippet: We propose TacFiLM, a lightweight modality-fusion approach that integrates visual-tactile signals into vision-language-action (VLA) models. candidates.title: TRAP: Hijacking VLA CoT-Reasoning via Adversarial Patches

candidates.abstract_snippet: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models show strong generalization for robotic control, but finetuning them with reinforcement learning (RL) is constrained by the high cost and safety risks of real-world interaction. candidates.title: ThermoAct:Thermal-Aware Vision-Language-Action Models for Robotic Perception and Decision-Making

candidates.title: Beyond Attention Magnitude: Leveraging Inter-layer Rank Consistency for Efficient Vision-Language-Action Models

candidates.title: Gaze-Regularized Vision-Language-Action Models for Robotic Manipulation

candidates.abstract_snippet: The development of embodied AI systems is increasingly constrained by the availability and structure of physical interaction data. candidates.title: ForceVLA2: Unleashing Hybrid Force-Position Control with Force Awareness for Contact-Rich Manipulation

candidates.title: X-World: Controllable Ego-Centric Multi-Camera World Models for Scalable End-to-End Driving

candidates.title: Fast-dVLA: Accelerating Discrete Diffusion VLA to Real-Time Performance
